apple: a small stem at the top
a circular indentation at the bottom
white or cream-colored flesh when cut open.
aquarium_fish: Often found in water or an aquarium setting
May be seen swimming with other fish
May have unique patterns or markings on their bodies.
baby: lack of teeth or few teeth
may be crawling or being held by an adult
may have a pacifier or baby bottle nearby.
bear: fur, which can be black, brown, white, or a combination
a hump on the back (for some species like the grizzly bear)
bed: mattress with sheets, blankets, and pillows
positioned against a wall or in a corner of a room
bee: stinger at the end of the body
beetle: two pairs of wings (one pair is hard and covers the second pair)
segmented body with three parts: head, thorax, and abdomen
can be various colors, often black or brown
often has a shiny or glossy appearance.
bicycle: a saddle for the rider to sit on
a chain connecting the pedals to the wheels
bottle: a cap or cork on top
can be transparent, translucent or opaque
bowl: typically made of ceramic, glass, plastic or metal
may contain food or other items
may have a rim around the top edge
can come in various sizes and colors.
boy: clothing typically associated with boys (like trousers, t-shirts, etc.)
less curvy body shape compared to girls of the same age.
bridge: spans over a body of water, road, or valley
made of materials like steel, concrete, wood, or stone
may have a flat, arched, or suspension design
may have railings or barriers on the sides
may have vehicles or pedestrians on it
may have lights for night visibility.
bus: large doors for entry and exit
often has a company logo or route information
may have a destination sign on the front
may have a bus stop nearby.
butterfly: wings are typically held upright when at rest
they may be seen near flowers or plants.
cabbage: may have a stem or root visible at the bottom.
camel: color can range from light brown to dark brown.
can: a pull-tab or opener on the top
label or branding on the outside
often found in kitchen or grocery store settings
castle: located on a hill or other high ground.
caterpillar: hair or spines on the body
often found on leaves or branches
may have distinctive patterns or stripes on the body.
cattle: long tail with a tuft of hair at the end
colors can vary from black, white, brown, or a mix
often seen in groups or herds
often found in rural or farm settings.
chair: made of wood, metal, plastic or upholstered with fabric or leather
can be in different styles and sizes.
chimpanzee: walking on all fours or standing upright
often seen in groups or with other chimpanzees.
clock: numbers or roman numerals around the edge
two or three hands pointing to the numbers
may have a second hand that moves continuously
may be digital with numbers displayed on a screen
may be attached to a wall or stand on a surface
may have alarm buttons or settings if it's an alarm clock
may have a pendulum if it's a grandfather clock.
cloud: can be various shapes and sizes
may appear in front of or behind other objects in the sky, such as birds or airplanes.
cockroach: relatively small size compared to other insects in the photo
couch: typically found in living rooms or lounges
can come in a variety of colors and patterns.
crab: a flat and round, oval, or somewhat triangular body
may be various colors, including red, brown, blue, or white
may be found in or near water.
crocodile: eyes and nostrils on top of the head
often seen near or in water.
cup: can be made of various materials like ceramic, glass, plastic or metal
can have various colors and designs
may contain liquid or other substances.
dinosaur: distinct body shapes (long necks, large tails, etc.)
horns or crests on the head for some species
scales or feathers depending on the species.
dolphin: a dorsal fin on its back
a long snout filled with sharp teeth
a blowhole on top of its head
flippers on the sides of its body
a tail fin, which is horizontal and moves up and down when swimming.
elephant: small tail with a tuft of hair at the end
flatfish: eyes on one side of the head
bottom side is white or light-colored
top side is dark and often has spots or patterns for camouflage
fins around the edge of the body.
forest: leaves or needles depending on the type of trees
possibly a path or trail through the trees
may include features like streams, rocks, or fallen logs.
girl: smaller and shorter stature compared to adults
may be wearing children's clothing or accessories
may be playing with toys or other children's items
may have bows, ribbons, or other hair accessories.
hamster: fur that can be a variety of colors, including white, brown, black, or a mix
house: a roof, which may be flat or sloped
made of materials like brick, wood, or concrete
kangaroo: a head that is small in proportion to the body
a hopping or bounding gait when moving.
keyboard: letters, numbers, and symbols on the keys
a cord or wireless receiver (for a computer keyboard)
may have LED lights or backlit keys
may have additional keys for specific functions (volume control, play/pause, etc.) for computer keyboards.
lamp: a switch or knob for turning the light on and off
can be made of various materials such as metal, glass, ceramic, or plastic
can come in various shapes and sizes.
lawn_mower: a grass collection bag or box
a cutting deck at the bottom
a pull cord for starting the engine
a fuel cap if it's a gas-powered model
a power cord if it's an electric model
leopard: yellow or golden fur with black spots
lion: long tail with a tuft of hair at the end
a broad, powerful jaw with sharp teeth
lizard: eyes on the sides of the head
some species have a dewlap, a fold of skin on their throat
some species have a crest or spikes along their back.
lobster: two large claws, one of which is larger than the other
a tail made up of several segments
a dark blue, green, or brown color (live lobsters are not typically red)
man: facial hair such as a beard or mustache
clothing typically associated with men, such as suits, ties, or trousers
absence of makeup (though this is not always the case)
maple_tree: leaves often have a distinct, palmate shape
leaves may be green, red, orange, or yellow depending on the season
bark is generally grey and can be smooth or rough
produces distinctive winged seeds known as samaras or "helicopters"
branches and twigs arranged in opposite pairs
can be a large, tall tree or a smaller shrub depending on the species.
motorcycle: a seat for one or two people
a tail light at the back
an engine, usually located in the middle of the vehicle
a fuel tank above the engine
foot pegs for the rider and passenger.
mountain: may have trees or vegetation on lower parts
may be covered in snow or ice at the top
may have clouds around or over the peak.
mushroom: can be various colors, but often white, brown, or tan
can be found growing on the ground, on trees, or on decaying material
some have a ring around the stem
orange: stem or small indentation at the top
may be cut open to reveal juicy, segmented interior
may be grouped with other oranges or fruits.
orchid: unique flower shape, often with a central "lip"
flowers may have spots, stripes, or other patterns
leaves are typically thick and fleshy
roots are often visible and may be aerial
usually grown in pots or hanging baskets.
otter: ability to be seen in or near water bodies.
palm_tree: leaves that are large, evergreen, and fan or feather-shaped
leaves that are clustered at the top of the tree
fruit clusters (coconuts, dates, etc.) hanging from the top
bark that is smooth or has a pattern of old leaf scars.
pear: pear shape, wider at the bottom and narrower at the top
may have small brown spots or speckles on the skin.
pickup_truck: front cab with two or four doors
a large, open cargo area in the back
a tailgate at the rear of the cargo area
a truck bed liner or cover (optional)
pine_tree: bark that is thick and scaly
branches that are often horizontal and whorled
tall height compared to other trees or objects in the photo.
plain: lack of trees or tall vegetation
often covered in grasses or low shrubs
horizon line is typically visible and far away
may have scattered bodies of water or grazing animals
plate: made of ceramic, glass, or plastic
can be in various colors and patterns
often seen with food on it
sometimes accompanied by cutlery or napkins.
poppy: a dark center, often black or dark green
leaves that are lobed or dissected
often found in fields or meadows.
porcupine: covered in sharp spines or quills
small head with a rounded nose
a tail, which may also be covered in quills.
possum: often seen hanging from trees or other structures.
rabbit: fur, often white, brown, or grey
large eyes, often dark in color
raccoon: black mask-like coloration around the eyes
often seen standing on hind legs
ray: gills and mouth located on the underside of the body
smooth or rough skin, often in shades of grey, brown, or black
eyes on the top of the body
may have spots or other patterns on their body.
road: lines or markings on the surface
signs or signals along the road
vehicles or pedestrians on the road
curbs or sidewalks on the sides
the road may be surrounded by buildings, trees, or other landscape features.
rocket: exhaust or flames coming from the bottom
large size compared to surrounding objects
presence of a launch pad or platform
logos or markings from space agencies or companies.
rose: green leaves with a serrated edge
the bloom often has a layered, circular arrangement of petals
the fragrance is not visible but is a known feature of roses.
sea: horizon line where the water meets the sky
varying shades of blue or green
presence of marine life or boats
reflection of sunlight on the water surface.
seal: often seen in or near water
grey, brown, or black in color
shark: gills on the sides of the head
tail fin that is larger on the top than the bottom
eyes on the sides of the head
pectoral fins on the sides of the body.
shrew: fur that is usually grey or brown
five clawed toes on each foot
skunk: small head with a pointed snout
distinctive white stripe or stripes running from the head down the back and tail.
skyscraper: multiple floors or levels, often with rows of windows
made of steel, glass, or concrete
often rectangular or geometric in shape
may have antennas or spires on top
located in urban or city environments
may have lights on during night time
presence of elevators or escalators inside.
snail: colors can vary but often brown or grey.
snake: comes in various colors and patterns
may be coiled or stretched out
may have a rattle at the end of its tail (if it's a rattlesnake)
spider: two body segments: a cephalothorax and an abdomen
may have visible spinnerets (where silk is produced)
may be seen in a web
variety of colors and sizes, but often brown or black.
squirrel: fur color can vary from red, grey, brown, black
often seen in trees or on the ground
streetcar: a destination sign on the front and/or side
usually painted in bright colors or with city-specific designs
sunflower: often seen in fields or gardens
seeds can be seen in the center when mature.
table: made of wood, metal, glass, or plastic
rectangular, square, round, or oval shape
sometimes has a drawer or shelf underneath the surface
can be covered with a tablecloth
items such as dishes, books, or lamps may be placed on top of it.
tank: a large turret on top with a long barrel
often in military green, desert tan, or camouflage colors
hatches or doors on the body of the tank
sometimes equipped with additional weaponry or equipment on the exterior.
telephone: buttons or touchscreen for dialing numbers
a base or cradle for the handset
a display screen for caller ID or other information
a power cord or battery compartment
tiger: large, forward-facing eyes, usually green or blue.
tractor: large, thick tires, often with deep treads
a driver's seat and steering wheel
a hitch for attaching other equipment
often painted in bright colors, like red, blue, or green
may have a front loader or other attachment.
train: smoke or steam if it's a steam train
distinctive logos or colors of a railway company.
trout: typically silver, brown, or rainbow colored
spots or speckles on the body
tulip: the flower stem is usually straight and tall
the flower can be of various colors including red, pink, yellow, or white.
turtle: a small head with a beak-like mouth
scales or scutes on the shell
colors can vary from green, brown, black or even multicolored depending on the species.
wardrobe: handles or knobs on the doors
often made of wood or metal
often found in bedrooms or dressing rooms.
whale: blowhole on top of the head
often seen in ocean or sea environments
may be seen breaching or spouting water from blowhole.
willow_tree: leaves are often a light green or silver color
bark is usually rough and gray
often found near water sources like rivers or ponds
can be quite large and tall
catkins (flower clusters) may be present in certain seasons.
wolf: grey, white, black, or brown fur
woman: Generally softer facial features compared to men
